intro: Caralluma retrospiciens, also known as 'Indian prickly cucumber', is a succulent plant species belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to aid in weight loss, improve appetite, and enhance endurance, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving metabolic function. The bioactive constituents of Caralluma retrospiciens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, particularly caralluma extract, which is responsible for its medicinal properties.
